parsed.clarification_question: Which focus should I prioritize for this paragraph? Choose one: A) Writing polish (sentence-level clarity, grammar, tone) only; B) Argument verification (logical consistency and claim–evidence alignment) without rewriting; C) Conceptual reframing of the contribution claim; D) A mix of A and B.

rationale.R1.2: The paragraph makes causal and mechanistic claims about sources of non-determinism and fixes, so checking the internal argument chain from observations to conclusions is central.
rationale.R1.3: Multiple reported metrics (ODR, BLEU variance, task-level consistency) are invoked to support the claims, so aligning those claims with the described measurements is central.
rationale.R1.6: A generic request to 'take a look at this paragraph' commonly implies expectations around sentence-level clarity, flow, and register.
rationale.RX.1: Because the request is underspecified, we should explicitly note assumptions and uncertainty about the intended focus.
rationale.RX.2: We must avoid overstating what the presented evidence justifies when assessing the claims in the paragraph.
rationale.RX.3: Work should remain confined to this paragraph and not drift to section-level restructuring or broader literature evaluation unless requested.
rationale.RX.4: Feedback should avoid encouraging practices that could lead to misleading evaluations or unsafe system changes.
rationale.RX.5: Any judgments should be traceable to specific sentences or numbers in the provided text to maintain provenance.
